# Test method taxonomy

| Code | Scientific name | Method | When to use | Minimum design |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| M-OBS | *Observatio basalis* | Observational baseline audit | Use before intervention to understand current performance and natural variance. | No variants; collect baseline metrics over 10–30 posts. |
| M-GEN | *Genoma creativum* | Creative genome audit | Use to classify competitor or niche patterns before producing experiments. | Tag 100–300 videos by form, hook, text, proof, CTA, length, metrics. |
| M-SVT | *Variatio singularis* | Single-variable variant test | Use when one creative variable can be isolated. | Same body, change one factor; 2–5 variants. |
| M-MPT | *Comparatio gemina* | Matched-pair creative twin test | Use when exact randomisation is impossible but comparable posts can be paired. | Same topic/body, paired variants posted under similar conditions. |
| M-ABR | *Comparatio binaria* | A/B split test | Use when the platform or ad tool allows direct split testing. | Control vs treatment with randomised exposure when available. |
| M-FAC | *Experimentum factoriale* | Factorial design | Use when testing combinations such as hook × first frame × CTA. | Two or more factors, two or more levels each; keep factor matrix explicit. |
| M-DOS | *Dosis responsum* | Dose-response test | Use for variable intensity such as text density, length, cut frequency, face percentage. | Test ordered levels: low, medium, high. |
| M-SEQ | *Series sequentialis* | Sequential batch test | Use when algorithms prevent simultaneous A/B testing. | Post a batch, read results, choose next batch based on pre-set decision rules. |
| M-CST | *Transitus platformae* | Cross-surface translation test | Use to compare platform-native edits across TikTok, Reels, Shorts, LinkedIn, etc. | Same thesis, platform-specific edit, platform-specific metric. |
| M-TRI | *Probatio nonsecutorum* | Trial Reels cold-audience test | Use Instagram Trial Reels to separate cold-audience appeal from follower bias. | Trial Reel first, then follower release only if metrics pass threshold. |
| M-SGA | *Assay quaesitionis* | Search growth assay | Use for TikTok Search, YouTube Search, Pinterest, evergreen search content. | Track 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d search traffic and query matching. |
| M-QCA | *Analysis commentarii* | Qualitative comment analysis | Use when comments reveal confusion, identity, objections, or social transmission pathways. | Code comments by theme, sentiment, question, objection, and share cue. |
| M-RPL | *Repetitio confirmans* | Replication test | Use before scaling a mechanism. | Repeat same mechanism with different topic, date, platform, or audience. |
| M-ROB | *Falsificatio robusta* | Robustness / kill test | Use when a tactic wins one metric but may damage trust or repeat audience. | Predefine kill metrics: completion drop, negative comments, low follow conversion. |
| M-PRE | *Registratio praevia* | Preregistered experiment plan | Use before important tests to reduce hindsight bias. | Write hypothesis, variables, metrics, thresholds, and decision rules before posting. |

## Method naming rule

Every experiment name should include one method code.

Example:

```text
EXP-20260421-YTS-SHRT-ATT.PRIM-M-MPT-FRAME5
```

Meaning:

```text
Date: 2026-04-21
Platform: YouTube Shorts
Mechanism: attention / first-frame salience
Method: matched-pair test
Variant set: five first-frame levels
```
